rubric_items.reasoning: The job's own reported state (STATUS=Complete, COMPLETIONS=1/1, log ending in 'spark_etl_pipeline_verified') and 4 running scaled workers are strong direct evidence the fix is durable and already held — the oracle also passed. However, the agent's specific claims about the applied fix (driver/executor memory at 1g, AQE skew handling enabled, 2G worker memory) were not re-verified with any command in this stage (no `describe job`/`get job -o yaml`/env check) — they were merely asserted from recollection of the prior stage rather than confirmed live. The conclusion is correct but the verification depth for those specific claims is thin.

Rather than blindly re-running full diagnosis and remediation on a recurring task, the agent first checked current job/pod/log state in one combined, targeted command to determine whether work was still needed — a logical, efficient check-before-acting sequence with no wasted steps or backtracking.

Only 6 read-only kubectl calls (job status, pod list, log tail) were made, zero mutations. This is the minimal, least-privilege toolset needed to confirm state before deciding no further action was required.

The agent made no changes at all — it correctly recognized the fix already persisted from the prior stage and avoided any redundant or unnecessary mutation, which is the smallest possible footprint for a task that was already satisfied.
